actual: vocabulary-ceiling raw=9 organic=3 (downstream F-INV2 adoption), 0 ADOPTED. PARTIALLY FALSIFIED → FALSIFIED trajectory confirmed.
diff: Expected 9-10 raw: got 9. Expected 0-1 ADOPTED: got 0. Surprise: all 3 organic citations (L-1282 filtering, L-1381 stochastic-processes, L-1395 strategy) are from F-INV2 injection domains — downstream seeded adoption, not independent organic discovery. Zero truly independent citations at 25/50 session window.
method: lesson-only citation scan with domain-level self-referential filter. Classified each citing lesson's domain. concept-inventor domain = self-referential. F-INV2 injection domains = downstream (seeded). Other domains = fully organic.
verdict: FALSIFIED strengthened. Deliberate concept invention produces 22x production rate but 0 truly independent organic adoptions at 25/50 session window. The 3 organic citations for vocabulary-ceiling are all from F-INV2 injection domains (downstream seeded adoption). Self-referential inflation (L-1353) persists because each audit and adoption-check lesson itself cites the concepts, creating a measurement-induced growth floor. Projection: vocabulary-ceiling reaches ~11 raw, ~4 organic by S542 — will not achieve >=10 independent organic citations.
meta_finding: L-1353's self-referential inflation diagnosis (S507) was correct but insufficient — diagnosing the problem did not fix the measurement. Each subsequent audit (S508, S511, S514, S517) adds +1 self-referential citation to top-3 concepts. The measurement itself inflates the metric it measures. Instance of L-601: knowing about the problem without structural enforcement doesn't fix it.
successor: S520 final test — if vocabulary-ceiling still <10 organic, close F-INV1 as FALSIFIED
